channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris visits North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath one day after Trump visited
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris visited North Carolina on Saturday as the state recovers from Hurricane Helene, arriving there one day after a stop in the state by Republican Donald Trump, who is spreading false claims about the federal response to the disaster.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the Butler shooting changed Donald Trump's campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he had spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: Many small Alabama cities have been beset by the same polarizing debate that thrust Springfield, Ohio, into the spotlight after former president Donald Trump promoted debunked misinformation about Haitian migrants.
item.dc:creator: Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Militants kill 6 Pakistani soldiers in a shootout
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Militants killed six Pakistani soldiers in a shootout, the army said Saturday, the latest unrest in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan where armed groups including the Pakistani Taliban are active.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden pledged to campaign hard for Harris. So far, he's been mostly a no-show
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On the last day of August, President Joe Biden was asked about his fall campaign plans. He promised a Labor Day appearance in Pittsburgh and said he would be “on the road from there on.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What happens to items surrendered to TSA?
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) said it does not confiscate items. According to the agency, if someone shows up with a prohibited item, they are given a choice on what to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces said they shot down a Russian fighter plane on Saturday while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dallas Uber driver seen on video punching passenger after dispute over legroom
item.description: A 68-year-old Uber passenger was punched in the back of the head in Dallas, Texas, after he complained about the lack of legroom inside a car, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New video shows Kentucky sheriff pointing gun at judge before alleged fatal shooting
item.description: Newly released surveillance footage shows when a Kentucky sheriff pointed his gun at a judge's head before allegedly shooting and killing him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: A well-known Asheville musical tradition has returned in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What renters should know about breaking a lease to buy a home
item.description: Renters can break their lease to purchase their own home but they should understand the ramifications of leaving early
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina county debunks rumors of 'bodies everywhere' and government seizures in storm-ravaged town
item.description: Rutherford County in western North Carolina debunked rumors this week about the mountain village of Chimney Rock that claimed there were "bodies everywhere" and the government might seize the town.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal authorities issue warning ahead of Oct. 7 attacks anniversary
item.description: The FBI and Department of Homeland Security issued a warning ahead of the one-year-anniversary next week of the Hamas attacks on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Bigfoot footage goes viral on social media
item.description: In what closely resembles a scene from a 1980s movie or a beef jerky commercial, new footage purportedly of Bigfoot has racked up millions of views on social media. The video was recorded in the Parallel Forest in Lawton, Oklahoma, on Sept. 27.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Mayorkas warns FEMA doesn't have enough funding to last through hurricane season
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says the agency tasked with helping communities recover from Hurricane Helene and other natural disasters does not have enough money to make it through the hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and COLLEEN LONG, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, KRON4

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Colorado county clerk Tina Peters sentenced to 9 years for voting data scheme
item.description: A judge ripped into a Colorado county clerk for her crimes and lies before sentencing her Thursday to nine years behind bars for a data-breach scheme spawned from the rampant false claims about voting machine fraud in the 2020 presidential race.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Motorized wheelchair stuffed with cocaine seized at Bush airport, CBP said
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) -- Unusually large cushions on a motorized wheelchair piqued the interest of officers at George Bush Intercontinental Airport last month in Houston, U.S. Customs and Border Protection said. Their suspicions were right.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, Fox 5

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Georgia father Obie Lee Williams spent every morning looking forward to a daily phone call from his daughter.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9
